denial_pattern_sources: wrong_benefit_category: CMS Essential Health Benefits Bulletin 2022; documented in Aetna member complaints
missing_iro_notice: CMS External Appeal Regulations 45 CFR § 147.136; ACA § 2719
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 06/10/2026 for Septoplasty related to Deviated nasal septum with obstruction (J34.2). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Clinical criteria for Septoplasty are not met per the cited utilization guideline module for Deviated nasal septum with obstruction (J34.2). The request does not satisfy threshold requirements in the referenced criteria set.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with guideline mis cite. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Deviated nasal septum with obstruction (J34.2). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Septoplasty recommended as the next appropriate step. Failed intranasal steroids 4 months; CT septal deviation 80%. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that guideline mis cite bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
